summary: Excellent intimate 1977 Bloomington show with tight performance and hot renditions of Tennessee Jed and Lazy Lightnin', recording presumed solid.
review: The reviewer raves about an almost‑backstage experience at the Assembly Hall in Bloomington, noting a small crowd that let them feel “part of the band.” The set is described as “right on everything,” with standout moments on Tennessee Jed and a jammy Lazy Lightnin', and no complaints about the audio quality are mentioned.

ai_rating.rationale: One glowing 5‑star review praises the musical performance heavily; recording quality is not discussed but absence of criticism suggests it is at least adequate, yielding a high overall rating despite limited data.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No specific audio comments; reviewer focuses on performance and atmosphere, implying the recording is acceptable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Intimate, high‑energy show with a small crowd that allowed close interaction; the band stayed tight and delivered a consistently strong set.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Felt especially close to Jerry; band was “right on everything.”
